rationale: The citing paper’s core contribution is causal identification of peer effects in disclosure decisions (including IV strategies for simultaneity/correlated unobservables and leader-vs-similar-peer influence), which closely overlaps with the cited paper’s documented contribution on causal asymmetric peer effects and identification under endogenous grouping/simultaneity. This makes the citation plausibly methodological/foundational rather than a generic mention. The match is a direct DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.47.5.883), supporting that this is a specific intended reference.
